commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory embedding provider routing for configured OpenAI base URLs and adds focused tests plus a local verification helper.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +48, Other +80. Total +178 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main prioritizes direct OpenAI memory adapters for the `openai` provider id, while the linked issue config uses a custom endpoint. I did not run a live endpoint in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed to choose the canonical overlapping PR, settle endpoint alias behavior, and require contributor-owned live endpoint proof.

target.title: fix: route memory embeddings to configured baseURL for openai provider
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93396 — When a provider named `openai` is configured with a custom `baseURL`/`baseUrl` (e.g. local Ollama via OpenAI-compatible endpoint), memory embeddings incorrectly fell back to `api.openai.com` instead of using the configured endpoint.

## Root Cause

Three separate locations checked for the direct OpenAI memory adapter without considering custom base URL configuration:

| Location | Layer | Issue |
|----------|-------|-------|
| `src/agents/memory-search.ts` | Config resolution | Already fixed — returns `undefined` when baseUrl detected |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts` | Runtime (memory-core) | `getAdapter` and `resolveEmbeddingProviderFallbackModel` returned legacy adapter unconditionally |
| `src/gateway/embeddings-http.ts` | Runtime (gateway) | `createConfiguredEmbeddingProvider` used direct adapter unconditionally |

## Fix

All three locations now check for `baseUrl`/`baseURL` before using the direct adapter:

- `getAdapter()` — checks provider config for custom base URL; skips legacy adapter when set
- `resolveEmbeddingProviderFallbackModel()` — same pattern
- Gateway `createConfiguredEmbeddingProvider()` — same pattern

Both `baseUrl` (canonical) and `baseURL` (camelCase alias) are recognized.

## Testing

- `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` — 2 new regression tests: openai + baseUrl, openai + baseURL (camelCase alias)
- CI will run existing + new tests

## Real behavior proof

Verification server included at `scripts/verify-baseurl-routing.mjs`. Start with:
```bash
node scripts/verify-baseurl-routing.mjs
```

This starts a local HTTP server on `http://127.0.0.1:19999/v1/embeddings`. Configure OpenClaw with:
```yaml
models:
  providers:
    openai:
      baseUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:19999/v1"
      apiKey: "test-key"
```

Then trigger a memory embedding request. The request arrives at the local server (not `api.openai.com`), proving the routing fix works.

### Verification server output (example):
```
=== baseURL routing verification server ===
Listening on http://127.0.0.1:19999/v1/embeddings

Configure OpenClaw with:
  models.providers.openai.baseUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:19999/v1"
  memorySearch.provider = "openai"

Then trigger a memory embedding request.
The request should arrive at THIS server, not api.openai.com.
```

**What was tested**: Config resolution, runtime adapter selection, gateway path.
**What was not tested**: Live Ollama instance (none available locally).
